section: Chapter: A Tale of Valour – Major Somnath Sharma and the Battle of Badgam

questions.question: Who was Major Somnath Sharma?
questions.correct_answer: An Indian Army officer and the first recipient of the Param Vir Chakra
questions.explanation: Major Somnath Sharma showed extraordinary bravery during the Battle of Badgam in 1947 and sacrificed his life while defending the nation.

questions.question: Where did the Battle of Badgam take place?
questions.correct_answer: Near Srinagar in the Kashmir Valley
questions.explanation: The battle occurred in the village of Badgam, which was strategically important for protecting the Srinagar airport.

questions.question: Why was Srinagar airport strategically important during the battle?
questions.correct_answer: Because it was the main route for sending troops and supplies to defend Kashmir
questions.explanation: If the enemy had captured the airport, it would have been easier for them to control the Kashmir Valley.

questions.question: How many soldiers were in Major Somnath Sharma’s company during the battle?
questions.explanation: Major Somnath Sharma led a small company of around 90 soldiers against a much larger enemy force.

questions.question: Approximately how many infiltrators attacked the Indian soldiers?
questions.explanation: The enemy forces greatly outnumbered the Indian soldiers, making the battle extremely challenging.

questions.question: What was remarkable about Major Somnath Sharma’s participation in the battle?
questions.correct_answer: He fought even though his arm was in a plaster cast
questions.explanation: Despite being injured, he insisted on leading his men into battle to support them.

questions.question: What message did Major Somnath Sharma send during the battle?
questions.correct_answer: He said he would not withdraw and would fight to the last man and the last round
questions.explanation: This statement showed his courage and determination to defend the nation despite overwhelming odds.

questions.question: What happened to Major Somnath Sharma during the battle?
questions.correct_answer: He was killed when a mortar shell exploded near him
questions.explanation: He died while helping his soldiers and directing the battle, showing great bravery.

questions.question: What award was Major Somnath Sharma given for his bravery?
questions.explanation: The Param Vir Chakra is India’s highest military award for bravery in the presence of the enemy.

questions.question: What lesson does the story teach?
questions.correct_answer: Courage, sacrifice, and dedication to duty are essential for protecting the nation
questions.explanation: The story highlights the bravery and patriotism of soldiers who risk their lives for the country.

notes.title: Major Somnath Sharma
notes.content: Major Somnath Sharma was a brave officer of the Indian Army who led his soldiers during the Battle of Badgam and sacrificed his life while defending the nation.

notes.title: Battle of Badgam
notes.content: The Battle of Badgam was fought in 1947 during the Indo-Pakistan conflict over Kashmir. Indian soldiers defended Srinagar from enemy infiltrators.

notes.title: Strategic Importance of Srinagar
notes.content: Srinagar airport was crucial because it allowed Indian troops and supplies to reach the region quickly.

notes.title: Major Somnath Sharma’s Bravery
notes.content: Even though he was injured, Major Somnath Sharma led his soldiers and continued fighting bravely against a much larger enemy force.

notes.title: Recognition of Sacrifice
notes.content: Major Somnath Sharma was posthumously awarded the Param Vir Chakra for his extraordinary courage and sacrifice.

facts.fact: Major Somnath Sharma was the first recipient of the Param Vir Chakra.
About 90 Indian soldiers fought against nearly 500 infiltrators in the Battle of Badgam.
Major Somnath Sharma fought in the battle despite having his arm in a plaster cast.
The sacrifice of Major Somnath Sharma helped protect Srinagar and the Kashmir Valley.
